claims.claim: Reserve compensation (tagmul miluim) is paid by Bituach Leumi under Chapter 12 of the National Insurance Law; employees are paid via employer reimbursement, while the self-employed and multi-employer reservists file a personal claim
claims.note: Bituach Leumi Reserve Service section. Tagmul is paid by BTL under National Insurance Law Chapter 12; employer-reimbursement track for employees, personal claim (form generated in IDF reserve personal area) for self-employed/multi-employer. gov.il/btl bot-blocks WebFetch; reached via browser snapshot.

claims.claim: The Bituach Leumi claim deadline is 12 months from the service date (and 12 months to appeal); the widely-cited 30 days is the war-period protected-money window during which the deposited grant cannot be frozen or seized, not a claim deadline
claims.note: BTL 'how to file' (אופן ההגשה) confirms the 12-month claim/appeal window. The 30-day figure is the protected-deposit window (IDF tagmulim page), corrected here from the common '30-day deadline' myth. This correction was a key research finding.

claims.claim: Effective 1 January 2026 the daily reserve compensation ceiling is 1,730.33 NIS (51,910 NIS/month) and the floor is 328.76 NIS (9,863 NIS/month)
claims.note: BTL reserve-service rates page (שיעורי הקצבה), 2026 figures. 2025/war-period figures were slightly lower (~1,689.83 max, ~321.07 floor per secondary sources); the course explicitly says 2025 figures were lower and directs readers to confirm current amounts. Year-indexed/volatile.

claims.claim: A self-employed reservist's daily tagmul is based on reported advance-payment installments (mkdamot) for the three months before service divided by 90, plus a 25% supplement, with a war-period rule using the higher of reported installments or the final annual assessment
claims.note: BTL reserve-service calculation page. Self-employed base = prior-3-month mkdamot / 90; +25% supplement; war-period higher-of rule with recalculation after final assessment. Reached via browser.

claims.claim: The current 2025-2026 self-employed business-support track is the Tax Authority grant for reservist business owners (מענק לעוסקים משרתי מילואים) for indirect economic damage; the older IDF support-fund scheme's application window has closed
claims.note: gov.il Tax Authority grant service page (403 to WebFetch, indexed/canonical). The live track is the Reshut HaMisim indirect-damage grant; the older IDF/support-fund scheme closed. Corroborated by amir-cpa.net analysis. Course directs readers to confirm current windows.

claims.claim: Eligibility for the Tax Authority reservist grant requires 30 cumulative or 21 continuous days of Order 8 (tzav 8) reserve service plus a turnover decline versus the prior-year matching period; exempt dealers (osek patur) receive fixed bracket amounts
claims.note: Tax Authority grant eligibility: 30 cumulative / 21 continuous Order 8 days + turnover decline; osek patur fixed brackets; turnover-decline-minus-saved-expenses calculation. Corroborated via amir-cpa.net. Windows close per service period.

claims.claim: The 2025 additional reserve grant (tagmul nosaf) is tiered by reserve days: approximately 1,452 NIS (10-14.5 days), 2,904 NIS (15-19.5 days), 4,356 NIS (20-36.5 days), and 5,808 NIS (37+ days)
claims.note: IDF miluim tagmulim page, 2025 additional-grant tiers. Course states '2025 tiers were approximately' and directs readers to confirm current amounts. Year-indexed.

claims.claim: A special grant (tagmul meyuchad) of roughly 133.33 NIS per full combat day applies to long combat service in the 32-60 day and 60 day-plus bands, with a personal-expense grant for shorter service (5-9.5 days)
claims.note: IDF miluim tagmulim page. Special combat grant ~133.33 NIS/full combat day in the 32-60 and 60+ day bands; personal-expense grant ~266 NIS for 5-9.5 days. Stated qualitatively in-course ('roughly'); year-indexed.

claims.claim: Reservists serving roughly 40 or more Order 8 days may receive a family grant of approximately 500 NIS per child up to age 14, rising for combat service
claims.note: Kol Zchut reserve-compensation entry. Family grant ~500 NIS/child up to 14 (higher for combat) for ~40+ Order 8 days. SECONDARY source (kolzchut, not a clean BTL primary fetch); course states figures as 'approximately' and 'indicative, confirm the current rule' to avoid over-stating. kolzchut bot-blocks WebFetch; reached via browser.

claims.claim: The Discharged Soldiers (Return to Work) Law of 1949 bars dismissing an employee for reserve service and requires an employment committee permit to dismiss a reservist; a 2024 amendment extended the protected period for those who served long stretches (60+ days in the war-period window)
claims.note: Kol Zchut entry on dismissal protection for reservists, citing חוק חיילים משוחררים (החזרה לעבודה), תש"ט-1949 and the va'adat ta'asuka permit requirement. The 2024 Knesset amendment (passed 30.5.2024) extended the protected window to 60 days for 60+ day servers in the 7.10.23-31.12.24 window. Law text on nevo.co.il.
